title: AI Proxy Dispatcher Config
description:
Configuration for the AI proxy dispatcher plugin. Exposes a unified OpenAI-compatible API and routes to LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama). Supports named targets for policy-driven routing (via the `cel` middleware), provider fallback on 5xx, and token count propagation into context for downstream middlewares.

Two configuration modes:
- **Flat**: set `provider` + `model` (+ optional `api_key`, `base_url`) for a single provider.
- **Targets map**: define named `targets` and optionally a `default_target`; the `cel` middleware selects the active target by writing `ai.target` into context.

The `fallback` list is tried in order when the resolved target returns a 5xx or a connection error.

TargetConfig.description: A named provider target: provider type, model, credentials, and optional custom endpoint.

provider.description: LLM provider. `openai` and `ollama` are OpenAI-compatible (passthrough). `anthropic` uses the Messages API with automatic request/response translation.

model.description: Model identifier sent to the provider (e.g. `gpt-4o`, `claude-opus-4-6`, `mistral`).

api_key.description: Provider API key. Supports `${ENV_VAR}` substitution. Omit for Ollama (unauthenticated local endpoint).

base_url.description: Custom provider base URL. Defaults: OpenAI → `https://api.openai.com`, Anthropic → `https://api.anthropic.com`, Ollama → `http://localhost:11434`. Use this for Azure OpenAI, self-hosted vLLM, remote Ollama, etc.

provider.description: Provider for the flat single-provider config. Required when `targets` is not defined.

model.description: Model identifier for the flat config (e.g. `gpt-4o`, `claude-opus-4-6`, `llama3`).

api_key.description: API key for the flat config. Supports `${ENV_VAR}` substitution.

base_url.description: Custom base URL for the flat config. Overrides the provider default.

timeout.description: Request timeout in seconds. LLM calls can take 10–60 s; set higher for long-running completions.

max_tokens.description: Default `max_tokens` injected into the request when the client omits it. Required for Anthropic (enforced by the API). Acts as a cost guardrail for OpenAI/Ollama.

fallback.description: Ordered list of fallback providers. Tried in sequence when the primary target returns a 5xx or a connection error. 4xx responses are returned directly to the client without fallback.

targets.description: Named provider targets for policy-driven routing. The `cel` middleware selects the active target by writing `ai.target` into context before this dispatcher runs. Example: `cel` sets `ai.target: premium` → dispatcher uses the `premium` target config.

default_target.description: Name of the target to use when no `ai.target` context key is present. Must match a key in `targets`. When omitted and no context target is set, falls back to the flat `provider`/`model` config.
